members.name: swarm-s1
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Analyze current product codebase and document existing features

**Task ID:** s1
**Title:** Analyze current product codebase and document existing features

Thoroughly review the current product's codebase, documentation, and existing features. Create a comprehensive inventory of all current capabilities, architecture patterns, and technical implementations. Document the product's core functionality, user workflows, technology stack, and any notable strengths or limitations. This analysis will form the baseline for comparison.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s1" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

members.name: swarm-s6
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Create comprehensive SWARMOUT.md with gap analysis, feature recommendations, and implementation plan

**Task ID:** s6
**Title:** Create comprehensive SWARMOUT.md with gap analysis, feature recommendations, and implementation plan

Synthesize all research findings to create SWARMOUT.md. Perform a gap analysis comparing current product features against all competitive products (claudecode, copilot-cli, OpenCode, roocode). Identify missing features and prioritize them based on competitive advantage and user value. Generate a detailed implementation plan organized into Milestones, where each Milestone groups related features. For each Milestone, break down specific tasks with clear acceptance criteria, estimated complexity, and dependencies. Include: (1) Executive Summary of findings, (2) Feature Gap Analysis section, (3) Recommended Feature Additions with justifications, (4) Implementation Plan with 3-5 Milestones, (5) Each Milestone should contain 3-8 specific tasks with descriptions, priorities, and technical considerations. Format the document in clear Markdown with proper headers, lists, and tables where appropriate.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s6" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.
